title: Corrected Statistical Analysis for KL-Constrained Baum-Welch
summary: Post-hoc evaluation of KL-Constrained Baum-Welch addressing 5 methodological gaps. (1) Table 1: absolute pp gains with 10K bootstrap CIs — k_true=1: +2.247 pp [−0.374, +7.165], k_true=2: −0.308 pp, k_true=3: +1.448 pp [−0.390, +4.859]; none significant at α=0.05. Previous output mislabeled relative % as absolute pp. (2) Table 2: partial ablation grid (k_true×T) with explicit footnote that BW-warm baseline is missing, preventing decomposition of constraint vs. iteration effects. (3) Table 3: bias-variance power-law refit with DoF awareness — 3-param fit to 3 T-points has DoF=0 (uninformative R²≈1); preferred 2-param fit fixes α=0.5 from theory (DoF=1, meaningful CIs). (4) Outlier analysis: seed 4 for k_true=3 contributes +8.09 pp mean gain; excluding it collapses k_true=3 mean from +1.448 pp to −0.213 pp — entire gain is non-robust. (5) Epsilon sensitivity: all 9 (k_true,T) cells show <0.5 pp spread across ε∈{0.01,0.05,0.1,0.5}, confirming KL constraint rarely active; gains arise from initialization differences, not regularization. Per-example fields: eval_pp_gain_eps001, eval_bw_accuracy, eval_kl_accuracy_eps001, eval_param_error_improvement, eval_eps_sensitivity_spread_pp, eval_kl_beats_bw. Discussion sections in revised_sections.md cover KL direction (Csiszár 1975) and proximal EM (Cappé & Moulines 2009). All 45 examples evaluated; schema-validated. Key aggregates: frac_kl_beats_bw=0.60, eps_flat_fraction=1.00, k3_pp_excl_seed4=−0.213.
